Module 4:
Launch List Secrets, Part 3
!
Executive Summary
It’s time to go deeper on lists, response rates, and lists in general. When I
was getting started I learned a lot about direct marketing and direct mail. In
general, the typical response rate is 1-2% in direct mail.
People always wonder what the typical response rate is for a launch. The
thing is it completely depends on your niche, list, offer, copy, etc. there’s
absolutely nothing typical. Generally something better than 1-2% is what
you should shoot for.
The best conversion comes from having a warm list of people that have
been getting valuable content from you, and now you give them a product
that they’ve been wanting. In PLF launches we’ve seen anywhere from
$50-100 per name/subscriber. Generally the average is $75-85 per name.
Measuring this is difficult because these launches usually have traffic
coming from JV’s, and they’re entering the sequence of PLC at different
points. Note: this number comes from the launch list not the main prospect
list.
The 3 biggest factors are your offer, copy, and list. With a launch list you
get a great list so that’s taken care of. The better your conversion is the
better you do overall.
!

• 	Example: if you know every name on your launch list is worth $100, then
you can tell your JV partners that on average each person they send that
joins your list will earn them $50 (50% of commission).
If you’re looking for partners or spending on advertising, the better your
conversions are the easier it will be. That’s why the sideways sales letter is
so important - when they go through the sequence they are incredibly value
This can be advanced, but I wanted to expose you to it because getting the
math right gives you huge power.
!
